workflow: Use a durable month bucket before adding records, so context can be cleared between incidents.
For each month, record the aggregate count source first, then process one lead at a time.
Promote a lead to data/incidents.json only when it has a stable public URL and enough public location/status detail.
After each promoted record, update processed_count, processed_record_ids, and used_sources_archive.

current_focus.next_lead: Continue May 2026 from May 6 onward; add source-backed public reports even when thinly sourced, but keep attributed language and coarse child-safe locations.

month_buckets.count_basis: Bianet February 2026 male-violence sheet reports that men abused 65 children in February.
lead_notes: The monthly source lists individual child-abuse leads by date. Process them one by one, preferring a case-specific source when available.

Record uses Manisa province centroid and omits child name/initials, exact district, neighborhood, historical home/missing-place, search site, family and suspect relationship details, pre-disappearance private-life allegations, forensic details, funeral, and media details; affected_child_count set to 1 because sources corroborate one affected child.

month_buckets.count_basis: No monthly aggregate source has been published yet for May 2026. This bucket tracks source-backed May 1+ public reports and legal updates processed individually.
lead_notes: May 2026 batch includes one-source and thinly sourced leads when a stable public source exists; wording remains attributed and status stays reported/investigation/unknown/dismissed as appropriate.
Fatmanur Çelik/Hifa İkra existing record was updated with May 4-5 Bianet trial-follow-up sources instead of adding a duplicate marker.

Records omit child names/initials, exact private homes, family-identifying details, schools/workplace addresses, hospitals, funeral/forensic details, media/image details, and other non-essential private-location details.
